stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • 1845: British explorer John Franklin and his crew embarked on an expedition to map the Northwest Passage in the Canadian Arctic.
  • 1846: Franklin’s ships Erebus and Terror became trapped in pack ice off King William Island.
  • 1848: After more than a year stranded in the ice, the surviving crew abandoned the ships and perished while attempting to reach the mainland on foot.
  • 1859: A search party led by Francis McClintock recovered written records confirming the expedition’s fate.
  • 2014: The wreck of Erebus was located in Queen Maud Gulf.
  • 2016: The wreck of Terror was discovered south of King William Island.
stem: The student wants to provide a historical overview of the Franklin expedition. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Francis McClintock wrote about the Franklin expedition’s fate after recovering records in 1859; decades later, in 2014 and 2016, the wrecks of both ships were located.
Setting out in 1845 to map the Northwest Passage, Franklin and his crew became trapped in pack ice in 1846 and perished while attempting to reach the mainland in 1848.
The Franklin expedition has inspired an 1859 search effort, a 2014 discovery, and a 2016 discovery.
In 1845, the Franklin expedition sailed to the Canadian Arctic, where, in 1848, they rescued the crews of two ships that had sunk, Erebus and Terror.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence provides a historical overview of the Franklin expedition, noting that the explorers set out in 1845, became trapped in pack ice in 1846, and perished in 1848.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes events inspired by the expedition; it doesn’t provide an overview of the expedition itself. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence lists discoveries inspired by the expedition; it doesn’t provide an overview of the expedition itself. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence misrepresents information from the notes; Franklin’s crew was itself lost in 1848—they weren’t rescuing others.
